text.title: nodejs-fastify Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nodejs-fastify as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Not awaiting plugin registration. Registering plugins without await causes async race conditions where plugins are only partially loaded when routes run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without await, the plugin's registration hooks may not complete before the app tries to use decorated properties or config, leading to undefined references and runtime crashes.

failure-mode.description: Missing type safety in routes. Omitting TypeBox schemas or using inline plain objects without Type.Object() leads to loose inference and broken handler typing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: TypeScript cannot infer request/reply types without explicit schemas, causing unsafe property access and runtime errors. Loose types bypass validation.

failure-mode.description: Mixing reply.send() and return statements. Using both in the same handler causes double-response errors where Fastify tries to send the response twice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fastify's handler can return a value OR call reply.send(), not both. Mixing them violates the implicit contract and crashes with ERR_HTTP_HEADERS_SENT.

failure-mode.description: Forgetting fastify-plugin (fp) wrapper on plugins that decorate the app. Without fp, decorators are scoped to the plugin and invisible to routes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fastify plugins are scoped by default to prevent namespace pollution. The fp() wrapper explicitly breaks encapsulation so decorators are available app-wide. Without it, app.db or app.authenticate are undefined in routes.
